summary: Excellent soundboard capture of a rare two‑set night packed with one‑off covers and a marathon 22‑minute Lovelight.
review: The recording is praised for its crystal‑clear, newly uncovered soundboard quality, letting listeners hear every nuance of an unusually improvisational first set and a second set that bursts with classic Dead energy. Highlights include the stunning one‑time‑only covers (“How Sweet It Is”, “Are You Lonely For Me”), a 14‑minute “Smokestack Lightning” jam, and a blistering 22‑minute “Lovelight”. Reviewers are overwhelmingly enthusiastic about both the performance and the audio fidelity.

ai_rating.rationale: Reviewers consistently give 5‑star ratings, lauding both the show’s exceptional set (rare covers, long jams) and the outstanding soundboard quality with no technical complaints.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clear audio; reviewers describe the source as “outstanding” and note no drop‑outs or muddiness.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Two‑set show; the first set feels almost entirely improvised with a string of rare covers, while the second set builds momentum through classic numbers and culminates in an epic 22‑minute “Lovelight”.

band_member_comments.Bob: Weir’s vocals are largely absent on “Lovelight”, but he compensates with intensely driven rhythm guitar.
